body:
### Summary

Better have short npm package names. We used the underscore `@base-ui-components` because we didn't find a better option.

I have flagged it as a breaking change as it's kind of one, but also doesn't have to be truly one.  

### User requests

- https://x.com/Studio384/status/1869132827037770200

### Options

We are working on gaining access to the @base-ui npm org name. Once we get it. We should be able to migrate to it. https://www.notion.so/mui-org/ops-Trademark-Base-UI-88f653d8a8b64cac96fa7546638ecc3d?d=24e76446683d430381fdd0a65c03e3d5

comments.body: What's the point of having this as a GitHub issue? It's not actionable, as we've already contacted npm and can only wait until they resolve this. I can see a GH issue only when we actually own the org, to update the code.

> What's the point of having this as a GitHub issue?

@michaldudak

1. There will be a pull request, which will close this issue.
2. As a developer, I might want to leave this feedback as it confuses me. Now, I can find more information about this frustration.
3. If this gets enough upvotes, I could see us prioritizing this change earlier than we would have had otherwise (same on the opposite side).

labels.name: type: enhancement
labels.description: It’s an improvement, but we can’t make up our mind whether it's a bug fix or a new feature.

labels.name: breaking change
labels.description: Introduces changes that are not backward compatible.

labels.name: scope: all components
labels.description: Widespread work has an impact on almost all components.

title: [all components] Rename npm org name from @base-ui-components to @base-ui
